clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for wound infection concern documentation.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common General Surgery documentation scenario included for structured clinician-controlled notes; safety labels are conservative because it must not generate management advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct documentation workflow for clinician-selected details around wound infection concern documentation.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
High-risk specialty/workflow: documentation scaffolding only; requires conservative medical review before use.
red_flag_prompts: severe or rapidly worsening symptoms documented if assessed
abnormal vital signs documented if measured
new neurological, cardiorespiratory, or systemic concern documented if assessed
clinician concern requiring escalation documented if present

onset/duration documented if discussed
severity/impact on function documented if discussed
associated symptoms reviewed if relevant
relevant negatives documented if assessed
patient concerns or goals documented if discussed
clinician-led risk context documented
scope limited to documentation scaffolding

history_draft.text: Patient presents for wound infection concern documentation documentation. [Onset/duration]. [Symptom context]. [Associated symptoms reviewed]. [Relevant negatives]. [Patient concerns/goals]. [Additional history].

clinical_blockers: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Operation, wound dimensions, drainage, collection, tissue viability, microbiology, laboratory or imaging results, diagnosis, medicine, drainage or debridement, escalation and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for surgical wound infection concern.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
